advanced to a different blocker at `lib.rs:1404`.

**Root cause:** `exp1` at `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.
rs:2892` parsed an expression and then asserted `e.k == ExprKind::NonReloc`, but the `lu
aK_exp2nextreg` equivalent call was left as a `TODO(port)` comment. With the call skipped, `e.
k` stayed at whatever the parser set (e.g. `ExprKind::KInt` for the literal `1` in `for i=1,3`), t

ripping the assertion. The call chain was `forstat` → `fornum` → `exp1`.

1404` — `index out of bounds: the len is 1 but the index is 1`. A different (and progressing) failure; the loop will pick this up on the
next iteration. No temporary `eprintln!` added; nothing to remove.

content.text:
The original panic at `lib.rs:2897` is gone; we've advanced to a different blocker at `lib.rs:1404`.

**Root cause:** `exp1` at `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2892` parsed an expression and then asserted `e.k == ExprKind::NonReloc`, but the `luaK_exp2nextreg` equivalent call was left as a `TODO(port)` comment. With the call skipped, `e.k` stayed at whatever the parser set (e.g. `ExprKind::KInt` for the literal `1` in `for i=1,3`), tripping the assertion. The call chain was `forstat` → `fornum` → `exp1`.

**Fix:** Replaced the TODO with a real call to `cg_exp_to_next_reg(ls.fs.as_mut().unwrap(), ls.linenumber, &mut e)?` — the in-crate codegen helper that already exists at `lib.rs:1025` and correctly sets `e.k = ExprKind::NonReloc` after emitting `LoadI`/`LoadK`/`Move` as needed. Same calling convention as the four other in-crate callers (e.g. line 2447).

**New error:** `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1404` — `index out of bounds: the len is 1 but the index is 1`. A different (and progressing) failure; the loop will pick this up on the next iteration. No temporary `eprintln!` added; nothing to remove.
